data.title: HOTEL & SHOP PLUS: Redefining the Future of Hospitality and Commercial Spaces
data.description: In Shanghai, the HOTEL & SHOP PLUS event organized by IM Sinoexpo wrapped up successfully at the Shanghai New International Expo Center running from March 31 to April 3, 2026. Going beyond a typical trade show, this premier platform provided insights and innovations for the industry.

data.title: Oil prices increase as stocks go up before U.S. inflation report. Truce with Iran in doubt, eyes on inflation data.
data.description: Investors are closely monitoring the release of U.S. government data on inflation as a temporary halt in the Iran war encounters several obstacles. The temporary cease-fire in the conflict with Iran is facing challenges, with significant attention on the inflation figures from the U.S. authorities.

data.title: HEINEKEN sells Bralima in the Democratic Republic of Congo and establishes long-term brand partnership
data.description: In a recent business deal, HEINEKEN has announced the sale of Bralima, its subsidiary in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The transaction involves a significant amount of money, with the exact numbers undisclosed. However, in the aftermath of this sale, HEINEKEN has forged a lasting brand partnership in the region, signaling its commitment to maintaining a presence in the African market. This strategic move is part of HEINEKEN's larger business strategy to strengthen its operations in key markets and enhance its brand visibility worldwide.

data.title: Warburg Pincus Establishes European Defence Investment Platform
data.description: Warburg Pincus, a global growth investing private equity firm, has launched a new European defence investment platform in London and Berlin. The firm believes the sector is experiencing consistent structural growth and strong policy backing in the region. MEAG, the asset manager of Munich Re Group, has committed as an early supporter of the strategy. This new platform aims to enhance existing investments and is expected to attract significant interest, with initial backing from a major asset management firm.

data.title: Albertsons Gears Up For Q4 Print; Here Are The Recent Forecast Changes From Wall Street's Most Accurate Analysts
data.description: Albertsons Companies, Inc. will announce its Q4 earnings on April 14. Analysts predict the company to report earnings of 44 cents per share, a decrease from 46 cents in the previous year. The anticipated revenue for the quarter is $20.47 billion, up from $18.8 billion last year. Allison Pinkham was appointed as the EVP and chief human resources officer on Jan. 26. The company's shares dropped by 1.4% to $17.47. For more details, readers can check the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga for the latest updates from analysts.

data.title: WEPACK 2026 to Showcase the Future of Packaging with Eight Integrated Shows in Shenzhen
data.description: The WEPACK 2026 World Expo of Packaging Industry, managed by RX China, is scheduled to be held from April 15 to 17, 2026, at the Shenzhen World Exhibition & Convention Center (Bao'an) in Shanghai. Recognized as a premier international gathering for the packaging industry, the event promises to showcase the latest advancements and trends in the sector.

data.title: ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd. Announces Monthly Net Revenues*
data.description: As of April 10, 2026, ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd. (NYSE: ASX, TAIEX: 3711, "ASEH") revealed its unaudited consolidated net revenues for March and the first quarter of 2025. The sequential and year-over-year comparison for March showed a significant increase in consolidated net revenues, totaling NT$ million. The Company's performance in the first quarter remained robust, with consolidated net revenues amounting to NT$ million, reflecting a noteworthy growth compared to the previous year.
